---
title: "The integrated substrate — interactive"
subtitle: "Four independent evidence layers, one gene core. Rotate it, and watch which genes each layer actually carries."
toc: true
---

The [harmonized substrate](notebooks/07_harmonized_substrate.ipynb) (NB7) merges independent
lines of evidence into one tagged network. This page lets you *see* that integration in three
dimensions: each evidence layer is a plane, and **every gene sits only on the layers that actually
carry it** — so the gaps between planes are as informative as the overlaps.

**The four network layers** (bottom to top):

- **Functional association (STRING v12)** — the broad net of co-functional links. Most genes live here.
- **Literature validation (OmniPath)** — curated, published interactions.
- **TF→target regulon (GRN)** — MITF / SOX10 / PAX3 transcriptional targets. Selective.
- **Mechanistic pathway (Raghunath 2015)** — the signed, directed melanogenesis model.

A gene threaded by a vertical line through all four planes is one where independent evidence
*converges*; a gene present on only one or two planes is supported more narrowly. Node size grows
with the number of layers, rings mark clinical (OMIM) genes, and diamonds mark Bajpai CRISPR
melanin-screen hits.

::: {.callout-tip appearance="simple"}
**How to explore:** drag to rotate · scroll to zoom · hover a gene for its layers and clinical /
functional flags · click a layer in the legend to isolate it. Best viewed on a desktop screen.
:::

```{=html}
<iframe src="interactive/substrate_multilayer.html"
        title="Interactive 3D multilayer view of the pigmentation gene substrate"
        loading="lazy"
        style="width:100%; height:820px; border:1px solid #e8e2d6; border-radius:10px; background:white;">
</iframe>
```

This shows the **162 genes supported by at least two of the four network layers**. A further ~530
genes appear in a single layer — almost all STRING-only associations — and are omitted here for
legibility; they are all present in the underlying data.

## The flattened view

The same convergence story as a single two-dimensional network — useful when you want every link at
once rather than the separated planes:

![**Where independent evidence layers converge.** The 37-gene core supported by ≥4 of six integrated layers; edge shade and width show how many independent layers back each link, and node fill shows layers per gene. Built in [NB7](notebooks/07_harmonized_substrate.ipynb).](notebooks/figures/nb7_convergence_network.png){fig-align="center" fig-alt="A flat network of 37 pigmentation genes with MITF at the hub; darker, thicker edges indicate links supported by more independent evidence layers, and warmer node fill indicates genes supported by more layers."}

## Reproducibility

The layered view and the flattened view are both generated from the committed NB7 substrate tables
by [`scripts/make_multilayer_interactive.py`](https://github.com/tinalasisi/pigmentation-gene-network/blob/main/scripts/make_multilayer_interactive.py)
and [`scripts/make_substrate_layer_figure.py`](https://github.com/tinalasisi/pigmentation-gene-network/blob/main/scripts/make_substrate_layer_figure.py).
The graph behind the interactive is also exported as a portable
[`interactive/nb7_multilayer_graph.json`](https://github.com/tinalasisi/pigmentation-gene-network/blob/main/interactive/nb7_multilayer_graph.json)
(nodes with per-layer membership, and edges).
